issues.title: bug: Logger can't format exception string when SDG training fails
issues.body:
bug: Logger can't format exception string when SDG training fails
**Describe the bug**
When an error occurs during the synthetic data generation stage in our E2E jobs, the logger statement can't always format the caught exception message correctly due to how the exception object is passed into the `logger.error()` function. Therefore, the E2E job fails before the true error message can be printed, which makes it difficult to uncover the underlying error. (Screenshot provided below.)

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Create a pull request
2. Have a maintainer trigger an E2E job

**Expected behavior**
The actual error message should be printed

**Screenshots**
![Screenshot 2025-02-10 at 10 31 06 AM](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1db02081-5edf-4a31-be88-b425edf336d4)

[Job logs for the above screenshot.](https://github.com/instructlab/instructlab/actions/runs/13239407579/job/36951385756)

**Device Info (please complete the following information):**
 - Hardware Specs: N/A
 - OS Version: N/A
 - Python Version: N/A
 - InstructLab Version: `main` branch

**Additional context**
N/A
issues.ground_truth_patch:
diff --git a/src/instructlab/model/accelerated_train.py b/src/instructlab/model/accelerated_train.py
index 26afef2be5..c6b234b20c 100644
--- a/src/instructlab/model/accelerated_train.py
+++ b/src/instructlab/model/accelerated_train.py
@@ -233,7 +233,7 @@ def accelerated_train(
             run_training(train_args=train_args, torch_args=torch_args)
         except (RuntimeError, KeyboardInterrupt, Exception) as e:
             if not isinstance(e, KeyboardInterrupt):
-                logger.error("Failed during training loop: ", e)
+                logger.error("Failed during training loop: %s", e, exc_info=True)
             raise click.exceptions.Exit(1) from e
 
 
@@ -273,7 +273,7 @@ def _run_phase(
     # pylint: disable=broad-except # training library emits a general Exception in lieu of a more specific one.
     except (RuntimeError, KeyboardInterrupt, Exception) as e:
         if not isinstance(e, KeyboardInterrupt):
-            logger.error("Failed during training loop: ", e)
+            logger.error("Failed during training loop: %s", e, exc_info=True)
         exception = e
     else:
         # progress onto the next phase on success
